artifacts.content:
Steve,

Finally sitting down after a long day and wanted to catch you up on the health circus and everything else.

You probably remember when Dr. McKay at St. John Clinic Rheumatology started me on allopurinol back on 3/18/19. Within about 10 days my face puffed up and I broke out in that diffuse itchy rash. Karen had to leave the Bank of Oklahoma downtown and rush me over to St. John Medical Center urgent care. They told me to stop it immediately and flagged it as an allergy. Between that and the penicillin mess back in June ’69 with Dr. Harold Pruitt in Broken Arrow – Mom hauling me to St. Francis ER covered in hives – I feel like my chart is just one big “do not give this guy anything” warning label.

Colchicine hasn’t been much better. During that flare in Feb ’22 I spent two days running between the bathroom and the recliner at the Hudson house. Mark had to pull me off that $4,800 rooftop job at Woodland Hills Mall and cover my 10‑hour shift. Since then I only touch the stuff if I’m desperate.

Now they’ve got me lined up for these KRYSTEXXA infusions every 2 weeks. Dr. Bennett thinks it’s my best shot since my uric acid was still 10+ last fall. I’m trying to schedule them on Tuesdays or Thursdays between 8–10 a.m. so I can still get some calls in for Green Country afterward and not fall further behind with TTCU on the mortgage.

Outside of all that, Liam’s obsessed with baseball. We mostly play “grandpa sits in a lawn chair and catches,” but I’ll take it.

Call when you get a minute.

Mike

metadata.subject: Catching up (and my cursed immune system)

artifacts.content:
Em,

Figured I’d write everything down instead of trying to remember it all when we’re wrangling Liam at Sunday dinner.

First off, don’t worry, I’m still steering clear of penicillin. That rash and hives I got as a kid back in June ’69 when Dr. Pruitt in Broken Arrow gave it to me for strep – the one where Grandma Pat rushed me to St. Francis ER – is burned into my brain. She still has the discharge papers in that old file cabinet. Make sure Liam’s pediatrician at least knows it runs in the family.

Gout-wise, things have been rough. The VA checked my uric acid on 1/10/25 – it was still 9.2 even with the daily 5 mg prednisone and the occasional colchicine I can tolerate. Dr. Ortiz is pushing hard for the KRYSTEXXA infusions every 2 weeks. He told me straight out he’s worried I won’t be able to keep doing full-time field work for Green Country Mechanical if the flares keep coming.

I’m trying to line the infusions up on days I’m not on those 12‑hour Saturday emergency shifts so I don’t miss my AA meeting at St. Mary’s Parish Hall again like I did on 1/13/26. Skipping that really threw off my head for a bit.

On top of that, my seasonal allergies went nuts last April when I was on that week‑long chiller job at OSU‑Tulsa. I was sneezing nonstop on the rooftop, eyes bright red. The safety guy, Rick, pulled me aside to make sure I was okay to be up there. I ended up taking two cetirizine instead of one just to finish the 8‑hour shift.

Anyway, how are you holding up with work and Liam’s school stuff? We can talk more Sunday.

Love,
Dad

Karen,

Sitting in the truck outside QuikTrip and trying to map out the next couple of months so we don’t go crazy.

With the interlock still on the truck from that June 3, 2024 DUI in Broken Arrow, I’ve got to be careful about early‑morning KRYSTEXXA appointments at the Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center on Yale. Mark Ellison at Tulsa County Court Services keeps reminding me that certain mouthwashes and even some nighttime meds can set off a false positive, so I may need to push those Tuesday/Thursday infusions a bit later in the morning.

On top of that, Dr. Bennett wants the infusions every 2 weeks, but I already told her I can’t do the first Monday of the month because of the Chapter 13 trustee payment meetings downtown. Ever since Green Country cut my hours back in Oct ’22 and we fell behind on the house, those meetings with the Trustee’s office are non‑negotiable.

I’m also trying to keep my AA meeting at St. Mary’s Parish Hall protected. Missing it on 1/13/26 when I had that 12‑hour on‑call shift really messed with my sobriety routine. I don’t want the infusion schedule bumping into that too.

Can you look at your chemo calendar from Hillcrest and our bills and help me block out some “no‑go” times? Between your oncology infusions, my gout treatments, and making sure someone’s with Liam a few evenings a week, we’re basically running a small logistics company out of this house.

I’ll be home in 20–25 minutes. Need anything from the store?

– M

Event: KRYSTEXXA Infusion – Tulsa Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center

Time: Tuesday, 2026-03-24, 8:15–11:00 a.m.
Location: Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center, S Yale Ave, Tulsa
Attendees: Michael Harris

Notes:
- This is the standing every‑2‑weeks gout infusion.
- Remind staff about the bad reaction at Hillcrest South outpatient infusion center on 10/3/24 – they had to stop the drip, give me epinephrine and Benadryl, and I almost passed out. Make sure premeds (cetirizine, Tylenol, IV steroid) are on board and they monitor closely.
- Ask them to document exact start/stop times on their paperwork if the visit goes over 2 hours. After the workers’ comp mess from my fall off the rooftop unit at Reasor’s on E 41st on 6/27/23, Linda Perez and the comp carrier scrutinize any medical time I’m gone from Green Country.
- Try to keep this under 3 hours so I can still make a couple of lighter afternoon calls and avoid more unpaid time off. We’re just now digging out after being 2 months behind with TTCU Federal Credit Union from missing about 6 weeks of work during that bad flare in Jan–Feb 2026.
- Double‑check that this doesn’t land on the first Monday (Chapter 13 trustee meeting) or conflict with Saturday AA at St. Mary’s.

Reminder: bring list of current meds (prednisone 5 mg daily, sertraline 100 mg, etc.).

metadata.title: KRYSTEXXA Infusion – q2 weeks
metadata.location: Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center, S Yale Ave, Tulsa

artifacts.content:
Personal medical/work notes – for my own memory

Allergies / bad reactions:
- Allopurinol – March 2019, started by Dr. Leonard McKay at St. John Clinic Rheumatology in midtown. Within ~10 days I had a diffuse itchy rash and facial swelling. Karen left work at the Bank of Oklahoma downtown and drove me to St. John Medical Center urgent care. They told me to stop immediately and marked it as a drug allergy.
- Penicillin – childhood reaction June 1969. Dr. Harold Pruitt in Broken Arrow gave it for strep; Mom (Patricia) rushed me to St. Francis Hospital ER with a bad rash and hives. She still keeps the discharge paperwork.
- Colchicine – February 2022 flare. Even low doses caused severe diarrhea and cramping. Stuck at 4821 S Hudson for two days, missed a full 10‑hour shift, and Mark Delaney had to reassign a $4,800 rooftop job at Woodland Hills Mall.
- Seasonal – grass pollen (esp. April). During OSU‑Tulsa rooftop chiller repair in April 2024, sneezing nonstop, eyes red. Safety officer Rick questioned if I was safe on the roof. Took two cetirizine tablets instead of one to finish the 8‑hour shift.

Gout treatment:
- Uric acid still 9.2 at VA on 1/10/25 despite prednisone 5 mg daily + intermittent colchicine.
- KRYSTEXXA 8 mg IV every 2 weeks per Dr. Bennett. Avoid first Mondays (Chapter 13 trustee meeting downtown) and protect Saturday AA at St. Mary’s.

Mental health:
- Seeing Dr. Alan McKenzie at Laureate Psychiatric Clinic since Oct 2024 for depression (chronic pain + Dad’s death in 2018). Sertraline increased from 50 mg to 100 mg daily. If I seem extra anxious before infusions, this is part of why.

Hi Sarah,

Hope things are calm at the rheum clinic today. I wanted to give you a heads‑up about a few moving parts before the next KRYSTEXXA round.

First, Dr. Ortiz at the VA checked my uric acid on 1/10/25 – it was still 9.2 even with prednisone 5 mg daily and the occasional colchicine I can stand. He’s on the same page as Dr. Bennett about staying with the 8 mg IV every 2 weeks.

I’m trying hard not to miss more work. After that fall off the rooftop unit at Reasor’s on E 41st back on 6/27/23 and the workers’ comp claim, Green Country’s HR (Linda Perez) and the comp carrier want documentation for any medical time. If the infusion at the Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center runs longer than 2 hours, I’ll need something that shows exact start/stop times so they don’t accuse me of abusing leave.

Money’s tight. We were two months behind with TTCU Federal Credit Union in Jan–Feb 2026 after I missed about 6 weeks of work during a nasty flare. On top of that, I’ve got the Chapter 13 bankruptcy payment meeting with the Trustee’s office downtown the first Monday of every month since my hours got cut in Oct ’22. I can’t schedule infusions that morning.

Also, I’ve been seeing Dr. McKenzie at Laureate since Oct ’24 for depression related to the chronic pain and Dad’s passing in 2018. He bumped my sertraline from 50 to 100 mg. I may ask more questions than most about side effects – that’s partly the anxiety talking.

Thanks for helping me juggle all this.

Michael Harris

Event: Karen – Chemo Infusion (Hillcrest)

Time: Wednesday, 2026-03-18, 3:00–7:00 p.m.
Location: Hillcrest Medical Center – Oncology Infusion Suite
Attendees: Karen & Michael Harris

Notes:
- This is Karen’s first chemo session for breast cancer. Expect 3–4 hours in the infusion suite. Bring her blanket, snacks, tablet, and charger. I’ll drive her and stay the whole time.
- Coordinate with Emily and Jason: they’ll handle Liam three evenings a week while Karen is in treatment. Double‑check their schedule so we’re covered on Mondays and Wednesdays.
- Tell rheum/infusion center: please avoid scheduling my KRYSTEXXA infusions on Mondays or Wednesdays after 3:00 p.m. so I can be with Karen for these oncology appointments.
- Need to juggle with my own stuff: KRYSTEXXA q2 weeks, AA at St. Mary’s on Saturdays, Chapter 13 trustee meetings first Monday, and ignition interlock requirements from the 6/3/24 DUI in Broken Arrow (watch early‑morning driving to the Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center on Yale).
- Also keep in mind depression follow‑ups with Dr. McKenzie at Laureate (sertraline now 100 mg daily) so I don’t stack too many medical days in the same week and risk more overtime cuts at Green Country.

Reminder: park in the garage, allow extra time for check‑in, and bring a notebook to jot down questions for the oncologist about side effects and what I should watch for at home.

metadata.title: Karen – First Chemo (Hillcrest)
metadata.location: Hillcrest Medical Center – Oncology Infusion Suite

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Harris <m.harris4821@example.com>
To: Karen Harris <karenharris@example.com>
Date: March 3, 2025
Subject: Krystexxa plan and work schedule

Karen,

Wanted to write this out so we’re both clear on what the doctors decided. The rheumatology folks are going to **continue KRYSTEXXA 8 mg IV every 2 weeks**, same as what **Dr. Laura Bennington** at **Tulsa Bone & Joint** used when I was in **Saint Francis Hospital** back in **February 2025**. They specifically said, “follow the same protocol as during your inpatient admission for the severe gout flare,” meaning the one where I was **unable to walk and needed IV opioids for pain control** overnight. Not my favorite memory, but I have to admit the regimen helped.

They also want the infusions set up **every 2 weeks on Friday mornings at 8:00 a.m.** at the **Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center, Tulsa**. I told them we have to be careful because of the **court‑ordered random alcohol testing** through the **Oklahoma Department of Public Safety** after my **DUI arrest on March 12, 2023 in Tulsa County**. Ms. Ramirez, my probation officer at DPS, keeps those test windows pretty random, so I’m trying to leave some flexibility.

On top of that, I still need to keep enough hours at **Green Country Mechanical Services, Inc.** but maybe back off the overtime like the ER doc suggested. Can you look at the calendar with me tonight so we can block out the Friday 8 a.m. slots and still work around the DPS tests and your dental office schedule?

Love,
Michael

From: Michael Harris <m.harris4821@example.com>
To: Emily Carter <em.carter@example.com>
Date: March 5, 2025
Subject: Why they’re sticking with the IV gout meds

Em,

Figured I’d explain what’s going on with the gout stuff so you don’t just hear bits and pieces from Mom. They’re going to **renew KRYSTEXXA 8 mg IV every 2 weeks** because I really don’t have other options. I had to stop **allopurinol and febuxostat** last year after that scare at the **St. John Medical Center Emergency Department** on **May 22, 2024**. That was when I **broke out in hives and had trouble breathing** – full‑blown **ER visit for a medication reaction**. The ER doctor there (don’t remember his name) said my kidneys were “**borderline**” and suggested I think about cutting back the overtime shifts at **Green Country Mechanical Services** so I don’t run myself into the ground.

Between that and the **inpatient admission for a severe gout flare** at **Saint Francis** this **February 2025**, where I was **unable to walk and needed IV opioids for pain control**, they’re pretty set on keeping me on the IV route for now. Not thrilled about more time in infusion chairs, but if it means I can still chase Liam around a little, I’ll take it.

I know you worry, but I’m managing. We’re lining the infusions up so I can still get some work in and not blow up the budget. I’ll send you the schedule once the clinic finalizes it.

Love you,
Dad

metadata.subject: Why they’re sticking with the IV gout meds

artifacts.content:
Title: Medical schedule – Krystexxa & psych follow‑ups
Date: Ongoing (starting March 2025)
Location: Tulsa
Attendees: Michael Harris

Notes:

– **KRYSTEXXA infusions every 2 weeks on Friday mornings at 8:00 a.m.** at **Allergy, Asthma & Immunology Center, Tulsa**.
   • Flag: avoid conflict with **Oklahoma Department of Public Safety** testing – random alcohol screens ordered after **DUI arrest on March 12, 2023 in Tulsa County**. Coordinate with Ms. Ramirez (probation officer) when test notifications come in.

– Backup option discussed with rheum team: if Friday slots fill up, may shift to **every 2 weeks on Wednesdays**, but must **avoid the second Wednesday of each month**.
   • Reason: standing psychiatry appointment with **Dr. Kaplan (psychiatrist)** at **Laureate Psychiatric Clinic, Tulsa**.
   • These are follow‑ups for **major depressive disorder** that really worsened after Dad, **Robert Harris**, died in **May 2018**.

– Reminder talking points for next visits:
   • Tell rheumatology: continuing KRYSTEXXA 8 mg IV q2 weeks is okay, but make sure all notes reflect that I cannot take allopurinol or febuxostat due to prior allergic reaction.
   • Tell Dr. Kaplan: mood has been a bit lower around infusion weeks; sleep still rough when gout flares.

– Personal note: keep Sundays open to check on Mom (Patricia) in Sand Springs and try not to stack medical stuff on family time.
